name: Stratholme
region: Northern Lordaeron, major walled city on the Eastweald grain and trade lines.

sensoryProfile.sight: The southern main gate choked with soldiers and horses, flour-white service lanes, smoke lifting in separate columns, torch lines cutting the street grid, and then, by chapter 18, a city shrinking behind the riders into separate plumes and one dark bruise on the horizon.
sensoryProfile.sound: Alonsus Chapel bells still trying to mark an ordinary hour, shouted orders clipped short under the gate, doors being broken in rhythm, screams spreading and then thinning, and finally the wrong quiet of a city too large to make so little human noise.
sensoryProfile.smell: Bread, wet burlap, horse piss, rank tallow, smoke, and the sweet-sour wrongness of the plague underneath every working urban smell.
sensoryProfile.touch: Crushed sacks in mud, rough service-stair stone, soot on the parapet, and city air hot enough to carry sparks up to the wall before distance turns the smoke cold in the riders' clothes.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: By chapter 18 Stratholme is no longer only the place where the Culling happens. It is the city whose residue keeps teaching after the riders have left its walls.

establishedFacts.fact: Stratholme is the next major city on the plague road after Hearthglen begins failing again.
Its granaries, bakehouses, main holding yards, and service roads are the most likely narrow intervention points left to the protagonists.
Arthas is already moving toward Stratholme at speed before Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia can reach it.
Stratholme is close enough to Hearthglen that a head-start rider team with fresh horses might still reach its feed points before the main column.
Its most important continuity features are now concrete logistical sites rather than abstract civic grandeur: granaries, bakehouses, service gates, and warehouse lanes.
The city may already be too far inside the grain cycle for the source plan to work, but not so certainly lost that the characters stop trying.
Stratholme's meaningful geography is now concrete: service gate, Market Row, warehouse lanes, church kitchens, and the formal main gate where the army arrives before entering in force.
The source-side burn plan works locally in Stratholme's warehouses, but the city is already too far inside distribution for a clean city-wide save.
Bread is already in hands, houses, and bodies before Arthas reaches the wall.
Live cult-aligned handlers are still operating inside the city and can coordinate or stiffen undead movement with whistle signals.
Stratholme's southern main gate functions as both ceremonial threshold and public command stage, which is why Arthas's order lands as theatre as well as policy.
The goods-side service lane can still carry civilians and scouts to an inner-wall stair above Market Row even after the main gate breaks into panic.
Market Row is the city’s broad commercial spine and the first district Arthas assigns to Falric’s companies.
From the inner wall, the city reads as separable districts with separate smoke columns and rotation lines rather than one uncontrolled riot.
Alonsus Chapel and the square around it remain audible and tactically central during the opening of the Culling, with bells sounding until the city’s silence overtakes them.
Arthas returns beneath the same gate on foot after the purge, washed clean enough to make the city look more stained by contrast.
From the road out, Stratholme remains visible as separate smoke columns flattening into a horizon stain rather than disappearing into abstract distance.
The city's city-scale residue differs from earlier plague sites: the bread-signature can fade with distance while the deeper answering pattern still remains legible in Jaina's body and notes.

By chapter 19 Stratholme remains an active horizon presence even off-site: its smoke is still visible from the camp south of the city and continues to function as proof, pressure, and refusal of any return to ordinary aftermath.

eventsHere.event: Chapter 14 turns Stratholme from future destination into immediate objective, with Jaina outlining how its feed points might still be disrupted before the plague fully settles deeper into the city.
Stratholme becomes the immediate objective of both Arthas’s main march and Jaina’s smaller feed-point intervention run.
Jaina successfully ruins contaminated stock in multiple warehouses and proves the source plan works locally inside the city.
The chapter establishes that the city is still too far gone for a clean rescue because contamination has already moved through kitchens, bread, households, and bodies.
Jaina reports the truth at the main gate to Arthas and Uther before the city is entered in force.
Arthas turns Jaina’s report into the public order to cull the city and breaks openly with both Uther and Jaina at the main gate.
Sylvanas, Jaina, and Cyndia escape the gate crush through the service lane and reach the inner wall above Market Row with help from a fleeing grain carter.
From the wall, the women witness the Culling as a district-by-district military process rather than a chaotic riot.
Arthas emerges below the wall after the purge with washed hands, giving the chapter its final image of Stratholme.
The riders leave Stratholme by the south road, carrying its smoke, its silence, and the new inference that the city has exposed a deeper source model than contaminated grain alone.
In chapter 19 Stratholme continues to dominate the road after the riders have left it: the city's smoke frames the camp, the decision to go north, and both institutional severances.
